Literal: namāmī = I bow; īśam = Lord; īśāna = Īśāna (the highest form of Śiv); nirvāṇa = mokṣa (liberation); rūpaṃ = form.
Meaning1: I bow to Lord Īśāna, whose form is Nirvāṇa.
Meaning2: I bow to Lord Īśāna, the embodiment of blissful liberation.

Literal: vibhum - the all-pervading; vyāpakam - the omnipresent; brahma - Brahman (absolute-reality); veda - knowledge; svarūpam - one's own form.
Meaning1: The all-pervading, the omnipresent, whose own form is the knowledge of Brahman.
Meaning2: He is all-pervading and omnipresent, whose self-essence is the knowledge of the absolute.
